techniques.evidence_quote: the attacker targeted the credentials to the databases, and created Linux instances with networking connectivity to said databases
the attacker(s) had access to privileged credentials that were previously stored in the LastPass account of a Ubiquiti IT employee
They were able to get cryptographic secrets for single sign-on cookies and remote access
gained root administrator access to all Ubiquiti AWS accounts, including all S3 data buckets, all application logs, all databases
full source code control contents, and signing keys exfiltration
The attacker, who unsuccessfully attempted to extort the company by threatening to release stolen source code and specific IT credentials

attack_chain.description: The attacker (alleged insider / former IT employee) obtained privileged AWS credentials stored in a Ubiquiti IT employee's LastPass password manager account, providing the initial foothold.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the attacker(s) had access to privileged credentials that were previously stored in the LastPass account of a Ubiquiti IT employee

attack_chain.description: Using the harvested credentials, the attacker authenticated to Ubiquiti's AWS environment as a privileged/root administrator, gaining access to all AWS accounts.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: gained root administrator access to all Ubiquiti AWS accounts, including all S3 data buckets, all application logs, all databases, all user database credentials, and secrets required to forge single sign-on (SSO) cookies

attack_chain.description: With root-level access, the attacker enumerated Ubiquiti's full cloud footprint, identifying S3 buckets, application logs, databases, and credentials stored within the environment.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: gained root administrator access to all Ubiquiti AWS accounts, including all S3 data buckets, all application logs, all databases

attack_chain.description: The attacker exfiltrated cryptographic secrets used to forge SSO cookies, enabling the ability to remotely authenticate to Ubiquiti cloud-managed devices globally without credentials.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: They were able to get cryptographic secrets for single sign-on cookies and remote access

attack_chain.description: The attacker provisioned Linux compute instances with direct network connectivity to Ubiquiti's databases, establishing persistent access infrastructure within the AWS environment.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the attacker targeted the credentials to the databases, and created Linux instances with networking connectivity to said databases

attack_chain.description: The attacker accessed and collected data from S3 buckets, application logs, databases, user database credentials, source code repositories, and signing keys.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: full source code control contents, and signing keys exfiltration

attack_chain.description: After exfiltrating source code and IT credentials, the attacker attempted to extort Ubiquiti by threatening to publicly release the stolen materials unless paid.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The attacker, who unsuccessfully attempted to extort the company by threatening to release stolen source code and specific IT credentials

initial_access_vector: Privileged AWS credentials stolen from a Ubiquiti IT employee's LastPass password manager account (alleged insider threat — former employee)
persistence_mechanism: Creation of Linux EC2 instances with direct network connectivity to internal databases; possession of SSO cookie-forging secrets enabling persistent re-authentication
privilege_escalation: Credentials stored in LastPass already conferred root administrator access to all AWS accounts — no lateral escalation step required; immediate root-level access upon authentication
